input.prompt:
I'm migrating my MSTest projects from VSTest to Microsoft.Testing.Platform.
My repo has a single Directory.Build.props at the root that is imported by
both production projects (e.g. `MyApp.csproj`) and test projects (e.g.
`MyApp.Tests.csproj`). I want to set `<OutputType>Exe</OutputType>` and the
MTP runner properties centrally, but I obviously don't want to turn my
library projects into executables. How do I write the condition in
Directory.Build.props so it only applies to the test projects?

input.initialPrompt:
I'm migrating my MSTest projects from VSTest to Microsoft.Testing.Platform.
My repo has a single Directory.Build.props at the root that is imported by
both production projects (e.g. `MyApp.csproj`) and test projects (e.g.
`MyApp.Tests.csproj`). I want to set `<OutputType>Exe</OutputType>` and the
MTP runner properties centrally, but I obviously don't want to turn my
library projects into executables. How do I write the condition in
Directory.Build.props so it only applies to the test projects?

data.content:
Use an MSBuild condition on the project name (or the `IsTestProject` property that MSTest sets). The simplest and most common approach:

```xml
<!-- Directory.Build.props -->
<Project>

  <!-- Shared properties for all projects -->
  <PropertyGroup>
    <TargetFramework>net8.0</TargetFramework>
  </PropertyGroup>

  <!-- Only for test projects (naming convention: *.Tests) -->
  <PropertyGroup Condition="$(MSBuildProjectName.EndsWith('.Tests'))">
    <OutputType>Exe</OutputType>
    <EnableMSTestRunner>true</EnableMSTestRunner>
    <TestingPlatformDotnetTestSupport>true</TestingPlatformDotnetTestSupport>
  </PropertyGroup>

</Project>
```

**Key points:**

- `$(MSBuildProjectName)` is the project filename without the extension — available before any NuGet imports, so it works reliably in `Directory.Build.props`.
- If your test projects use other suffixes (`.UnitTests`, `.IntegrationTests`), broaden the condition or use multiple groups.
- **Alternative:** condition on `IsTestProject` — but that property is set *by MSTest's SDK targets*, which run **after** `Directory.Build.props`, so it won't be available there. Use `Directory.Build.targets` instead if you prefer that approach:

```xml
<!-- Directory.Build.targets (evaluated after NuGet SDK imports) -->
<Project>
  <PropertyGroup Condition="'$(IsTestProject)' == 'true'">
    <OutputType>Exe</OutputType>
    <EnableMSTestRunner>true</EnableMSTestRunner>
    <TestingPlatformDotnetTestSupport>true</TestingPlatformDotnetTestSupport>
  </PropertyGroup>
</Project>
```

The naming-convention approach in `.props` is the most widely used pattern.
data.reasoningText:
The user is asking about how to conditionally set MSBuild properties in a `Directory.Build.props` file so they only apply to test projects (ones that reference MSTest/MTP) and not production/library projects.

This is a knowledge question about MSBuild conditionals. The standard approach is to use an MSBuild condition that checks for a convention that distinguishes test projects — typically the project name ending in `.Tests`, or the presence of the `IsTestProject` property.
